compatibility_summary: JAXB and JAX-WS modules were removed from JDK after Java 11
Strong encapsulation may break reflection-based code

categories.recommendation: Review whether these javax APIs belong to Java SE, legacy Java EE, or external libraries before planning migration changes.
If the target runtime is Tomcat 10+ or Jakarta EE, plan a javax.servlet -> jakarta.servlet migration. If staying on Tomcat 9 or equivalent, verify whether keeping javax.servlet is acceptable.
Replace internal JDK API usage with supported public alternatives as these are high-risk on newer Java versions.

suggested_plan: Upgrade the build configuration and runtime baseline to the target Java version.
Review XML-related APIs and add explicit dependencies where JDK-bundled components were removed.
Evaluate the target servlet container and decide whether javax.servlet must be migrated to jakarta.servlet.
Replace internal JDK API usages with supported public alternatives before final upgrade validation.
Run compilation, tests, and smoke validation on the upgraded target runtime.

architecture_insights: Project uses javax.servlet both in code and dependencies → Jakarta migration likely required for modern runtimes
Legacy Java version detected with XML-heavy usage → High migration complexity expected
Very old dependencies detected → Dependency upgrade required before or during migration
Large Java version jump detected → Incremental upgrade path recommended (8 → 11 → 17)

top_fix_candidates.title: Plan javax.servlet to jakarta.servlet migration
top_fix_candidates.reason: Servlet APIs appear in code and are a common blocker for modern Jakarta-based runtimes.
top_fix_candidates.recommendation: If targeting Tomcat 10+ or Jakarta EE, migrate javax.servlet imports and dependencies to jakarta.servlet.

top_fix_candidates.title: Upgrade legacy servlet dependencies
top_fix_candidates.reason: Old servlet dependencies may block runtime modernization or container upgrades.
top_fix_candidates.recommendation: Review and replace old servlet artifacts such as servlet-api 2.5 / javax.servlet-api 3.x according to target container.

top_fix_candidates.title: Replace internal JDK API usages
top_fix_candidates.reason: Internal or vendor-specific APIs are risky on newer Java versions and can break at runtime.
top_fix_candidates.recommendation: Replace com.sun.* / sun.* usages with supported public APIs before final migration validation.

top_fix_candidates.title: Review general javax usage
top_fix_candidates.reason: There is broad javax usage across the codebase, but not all of it requires migration.
top_fix_candidates.recommendation: Classify javax usages into Java SE, external libs, and Jakarta migration candidates before planning large-scale code changes.
